source_pr.title: planner: fix window which depends on another
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

fix window function: A window which depends on another cannot define partitioning
[#11007](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/11007)


### What is changed and how it works?

Modify the order of error judgments

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test
 - Integration test

symptoms: sql: "SELECT FIRST_VALUE(a) RESPECT NULLS OVER (w1 PARTITION BY b ORDER BY b ASC, a DESC ROWS 2 PRECEDING) AS 'first_value', a, b FROM ( SELECT a, b FROM `t` ) as t WINDOW w1 AS (PARTITION BY b ORDER BY b ASC, a ASC );", (planner/core/logical_plan_test.go)
failure_signature: sql: "SELECT FIRST_VALUE(a) RESPECT NULLS OVER (w1 PARTITION BY b ORDER BY b ASC, a DESC ROWS 2 PRECEDING) AS 'first_value', a, b FROM ( SELECT a, b FROM `t` ) as t WINDOW w1 AS (PARTITION BY b ORDER BY b ASC, a ASC );", (planner/core/logical_plan_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: sql: "SELECT FIRST_VALUE(a) RESPECT NULLS OVER (w1 PARTITION BY b ORDER BY b ASC, a DESC ROWS 2 PRECEDING) AS 'first_value', a, b FROM ( SELECT a, b FROM `t` ) as t WINDOW w1 AS (PARTITION BY b ORDER BY b ASC, a ASC );", (planner/core/logical_plan_test.go)
